AboutStory: When I finished high school, I still wasn't sure which path to follow. I started with machining mechanics and then specialized in lathe machining and tooling, building a solid foundation in the industrial field. Despite the effort, I realized that was not the path I wanted for the long term. Soon after, I began my professional journey in the food industry at Farmina Pet Foods, where I worked for four years. During that period, in my second year at the company, I decided to resume my studies and started a technical course in Mechatronics to expand my knowledge and grow technically. As soon as I graduated, I joined a hospital equipment maintenance company. It was an extremely enriching phase where I improved my mechanical and electromechanical skills, while also developing technical responsibility and problem-solving capacity in critical environments. Over time, however, the routine of constant travel—often spending weeks away from home—started to weigh on me. I realized I wanted to be closer to my family and, at the same time, felt I needed a new direction in my career. That was when I decided to start a degree in Systems Analysis and Development. Since the travel routine was no longer compatible with this goal, I looked for a new opportunity and joined Concept, where I could better align my professional life with my studies. In the fourth semester of my degree, I earned an internship opportunity at Treetech Tecnologia, where I currently work. It has been an amazing experience. Beyond technical learning, I have the privilege of working with experienced and supportive professionals who contribute not only to my growth inside the company, but also to my development as a professional. Today, looking at my journey, I see that every step was essential to build the technical foundation, maturity, and systems perspective I bring to the technology field.
sobreMim: My name is Bruno dos Santos Cardoso, I am 28 years old and I am a student of Systems Analysis and Development at the Federal Institute of São Paulo, Bragança Paulista campus. Currently, I work as a Middleware Engineering intern at the technology company Treetech, where I develop solutions focused on embedded systems and software integration.
InfoFarmina: I started my professional journey at Farmina as a Production Assistant. From the beginning, I stood out for my commitment, proactivity, and dedication to production line activities. In a short time, I started supporting the line leader in operational and organizational tasks, and later I was assigned to replace him during his vacation period. After about one year, I was promoted to Junior Operator, taking responsibility for a production line. After the department modernization and migration to an automated system, I advanced to Full Operator, operating and monitoring the new automated production process. Once I realized I had reached the limit of technical growth in the operational role, I decided to invest in my professional qualification by starting the Technical Course in Mechatronics, focused on automation and industrial technology.
InfoTreetech: I currently work as an intern at Treetech Tecnologia, and it has been an extremely enriching experience. I am part of a project where I am responsible for front-end development using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The project has a strict size limit in KB because the application must run on a microcontroller board. This constraint has provided major learning opportunities, especially around code optimization, reducing resource consumption, and building efficiently in hardware-limited environments. This experience has expanded my technical knowledge and strengthened my ability to deliver high-performance, well-structured solutions even under technical restrictions.
InfoStarclave: After completing the Technical Course in Mechatronics, I joined Starclave, where I worked for about two years in technical maintenance. During that period, I performed preventive and corrective maintenance on hospital equipment, carrying out diagnostics and interventions in electrical and mechanical systems. The role required high responsibility, technical precision, and availability for frequent travel, since services were performed on-site in different locations. The experience strongly developed my failure analysis, problem-solving, and technical autonomy. However, due to the intense travel routine and long periods away from home, I decided to direct my career toward Information Technology, seeking to stay closer to my family and pursue opportunities aligned with remote work and software development.
InfoConcept: It was a very positive experience. I participated in electrical panel assembly, performed panel updates and maintenance, and also worked on electrical infrastructure, including cable routing, inverter installation, and inverter programming.
AT_1_MSG: Training focused on conventional machining processes, with emphasis on operating mechanical lathe, milling, and grinding machines. Developed skills in metrology using precision measuring instruments, as well as interpretation and preparation of mechanical technical drawings. The final project consisted of manufacturing a mechanical rocket with a fixation base, applying machining concepts, dimensional tolerances, and surface finishing.
AT_2_MSG: Complementary course to Machining Mechanics training, focused on deepening conventional turning techniques. Developed skills in machining parts fixed between centers, manufacturing eccentric shafts (crankshaft), pulleys, and small gears. Applied concepts of dimensional tolerances, mechanical fitting, and different methods of fixation and operation on the mechanical lathe. Throughout the course, several practical projects were completed to consolidate technical learning.
AT_3_MSG: Training that integrated knowledge from Machining Mechanics and Mechanical Lathe Operator courses, focused on manufacturing and fitting tools and molds. Developed skills in precision machining, assembly, and dimensional adjustments. The final project was completed in a team with strategic task division to optimize deadline and process efficiency. The work consisted of manufacturing a plastic injection mold for a garlic grinder, applying concepts of cavity design, cooling, tolerances, and finishing to ensure part conformity after polymer cooling.
AT_4_TITLE: Federal Institute of São Paulo - IFSP
AT_4_MSG: Technical training with emphasis on electronics, industrial automation, and programming. During the course, I deepened my knowledge in electronic circuits, embedded systems, and control logic. I had my first contact with programming, starting with C and later Assembly, applied to microcontroller systems. As a final course project, our team developed an automated sorting conveyor for material separation (plastic and metal). The system used Arduino for logical control and sensors for material identification, enabling automatic separation. I also developed a simulation in Coldsys.io, implementing control logic in Ladder language (PLC), where products were classified by size, reinforcing industrial automation and discrete control concepts.
AT_5_MSG: Training focused on advanced Microsoft Excel usage for data organization, analysis, and processing. Improved skills in creating structured spreadsheets, using advanced formulas, pivot tables, and analytics resources to handle large data volumes. The course helped optimize processes, increase productivity, and improve decision-making through more organized and efficient reports.
AT_6_TITLE: Federal Institute of São Paulo - IFSP
AT_6_MSG: Technology degree focused on software development, systems architecture, and programming best practices. During the course, I gained practical experience with different languages and technologies, applying front-end, back-end, and mobile development concepts. As a team project, we developed a book forum, putting into practice the knowledge acquired throughout the program. The application included a Vue.js front-end, a Flutter mobile version, and a Java back-end using service-oriented architecture and API integration. The project involved database modeling, user authentication, and dynamic content organization.
PROJECT_1_DESC: I also actively participated in Coding Dojo sessions with my classmates, where we formed a study group to improve our programming logic and strengthen teamwork. During these sessions, we practiced solving problems together, discussed different approaches to the same challenge, and improved our ability to write cleaner, more organized, and more efficient code. Beyond technical growth, the group also aimed to encourage new participants to enter the programming world, promoting collaboration, knowledge sharing, and collective growth.
PROJECT_2_DESC: I also participated in the InterIF Marathon VII, held at the Federal Institute of São Carlos. The competition works with teams of three members using only one computer per group. During the marathon, teams must solve 10 programming challenges in the shortest possible time. Scoring is based on the number of correctly solved problems and submission time — the faster and more accurate, the higher the score. The experience was extremely enriching. Working under pressure, making quick decisions, and validating logic in real time brought a unique sense of challenge and achievement. For each solved problem, we received a balloon as a symbol of success, which made the environment even more motivating and competitive. Besides the technical aspect, the marathon strengthened my teamwork, strategic communication, and logical reasoning skills in high-pressure situations.
PROJECT_3_DESC: As soon as I joined Treetech, I was encouraged to deepen my knowledge of the tool used in the company's front-end development: Vue 3, together with the Vuetify framework. To speed up my learning, I took a Vue-focused course and developed a practical project: a Pokédex. The goal was to consolidate the concepts learned and apply in practice resources such as componentization, reactivity, API consumption, state organization, and layout structuring with Vuetify. The project was very important for my growth, as it allowed me to use several tools and best practices that are now part of my day-to-day work, making my development more organized, productive, and efficient.
